textPlain: Does this depend on sliding sync and their standalone authz server like Element X does?I'd love to use Element X, but Element abandoned the form of SSO my community depends on, and I don't really have an appetite to spend 12 hours of my free time standing up sliding sync, a separate auth server, migrating my users to the separate auth server, spending hours explaining to everyone that their credentials live in some other place now, and then migrating my custom server admin software to use OpenID connect. Oh well.

This is interesting timing because I self-host a Matrix server (Conduit) for some friends and we were lamenting the dearth of good apps. I'm currently using Cinny as a Safari app on desktop and PWA on my phone, and Element.The new Element design is nice.
The back button does not work on my phone, android.
The only design issues I ever had with Matrix was the protocol design. This is lipstick on a pig.
A little bit of slack here, a little bit of teams there and a smidge of discord. Really not sure about it.
Whats Bad with the protocol design?
From the post:> ...lays the groundwork for a more resilient technical foundationLet's hope the protocol improves
